title: Dense Bi-Encoder Retrieval and Domain Transfer · Program Brief: Rag Asymmetric Query Document
summary: A semantic search prototype embeds queries and documents identically without checking model instructions, returns top five from a large corpus, and has never tested domain or language transfer. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the asymmetric query document increment in dense retrieval candidate service. Learn…
objectives: Apply model-specific query and document prompts, prefixes, normalization, and maximum-length behavior.
Separate offline document encoding from online query encoding while preserving one versioned bi-encoder space.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-dense-retrieval-4
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-dense-retrieval-4.
# Case 1655: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def worked_rag_dense_retrieval_4(query_model: str = "retriever-v4", document_model: str = "retriever-v4", candidate_count: int = 50, relevant_rank: int = 4, domain_evaluated: bool = True, multilingual_evaluated: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "query4dekrow",
        "tenant1655",
        "indexworked41655",
        "prompt4dekrow1655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if query_model != document_model:
        return False, 0, "model-space-mismatch"
    if candidate_count <= 0 or relevant_rank < 1 or relevant_rank > candidate_count:
        return False, relevant_rank, "candidate-rank"
    if not domain_evaluated:
        return False, relevant_rank, "domain-transfer"
    if not multilingual_evaluated:
        return False, relevant_rank, "language-transfer"
    recall_proxy = candidate_count - relevant_rank + 1
    assert recall_proxy > 0
    return True, recall_proxy, "dense-candidates"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Apply model-specific query and document prompts, prefixes, normalization, and maximum-length behavior.

hints: For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Asymmetric Query Document model · Theory Rag Asymmetric Query Document, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Asymmetric Query Document precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Defend the Rag Asymmetric Query Document model · Theory Rag Asymmetric Query Document, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory RAG engineering team handles case 5053 during a regulated document deletion request. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate dense retrieval on one embedding space, candidate recall, domain, and language evidence; retain corpus, document, chunk, model, index, prompt, query, and evaluation version identity; then double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Apply model-specific query and document prompts, prefixes, normalization, and maximum-length behavior.

For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Asymmetric Query Document model · Theory Rag Asymmetric Query Document, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: Query and document text should always receive byte-identical preprocessing.
